title: RADseq analysis workflows – Paula Neuhaus
text:
This repository contains Quarto-documented scripts developed during the Bachelor’s thesis of Paula Neuhaus at the Institute for Plant Sciences, University of Cologne.
The workflows document RADseq-based genomic analyses of Hypericum pulchrum, with the aim of processing sequencing data, generating high-quality SNP datasets, and analysing spatial genomic structure among populations.

title: RADseq analysis workflows – Paula Neuhaus
text:
This repository contains Quarto-documented scripts developed during the Bachelor’s thesis of Paula Neuhaus at the Institute for Plant Sciences, University of Cologne.
The workflows document RADseq-based genomic analyses of Hypericum pulchrum, with the aim of processing sequencing data, generating high-quality SNP datasets, and analysing spatial genomic structure among populations.

title: RADseq analysis workflows – Paula Neuhaus
text:
Workflows

RADseq sequencing batch 1

HM_hpulchrum_batch1.qmd

Workflow for the first RADseq sequencing batch, including demultiplexing, read quality control, trimming, mapping, duplicate removal, BAM quality assessment, variant calling, SNP filtering, PCA, and SNP summaries.


RADseq sequencing batch 2

HM_hpulchrum_batch2.qmd

Workflow for the additional RADseq sequencing batch, analysed with the same general processing framework to extend the population genomic dataset.


RADinitio restriction-enzyme simulation

RADinitio_simulation.qmd

Simulation workflow using RADinitio to evaluate the RADseq restriction enzyme used in the experiment, KpnI, in comparison with alternative enzyme choices tested in silico. The workflow includes population simulation, RAD library simulation, mapping, duplicate removal, BAM/VCF quality assessment, filtering, PCA, and SNP counting.

title: RADseq analysis workflows – Paula Neuhaus
text:
Main analysis components

RADseq demultiplexing and barcode handling
Read trimming and quality control
Mapping to the H. pulchrum reference genome
BAM processing and duplicate removal
Variant calling and VCF summary statistics
SNP filtering and dataset preparation
PCA-based assessment of genomic structure
RADinitio-based restriction-enzyme evaluation

title: RADseq analysis workflows – Paula Neuhaus
text:
Repository purpose
The repository provides a reproducible record of the scripts used to process RADseq data and assess genomic differentiation and spatial population structure in Hypericum pulchrum.

title: In Silico
text:
https://catchenlab.life.illinois.edu/radinitio/
The following scripts are done on the example of the Hypericum pulchrum reference genome.


Path: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Scripts/radinitio_pop_10_5.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=rad_pop   
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=10G
#SBATCH --time=120:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_pop_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_pop_%j.err

# Create directories
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs

# Thread control
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK

echo "=== Starting Population Simulation at $(date) ==="

mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/

# Run population simulation only - FIXED genome path
radinitio --make-population \
   --genome /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta \
   --out-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ \
   --n-pops 10 \
   --n-seq-indv 5

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   echo "Population simulation completed successfully at $(date)"

   # Check outputs
   echo "=== Output files ==="
   ls -lh /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/

   # Check for VCF file
   VCF_FILE="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ref_loci_vars/ri_master.vcf.gz"
   if [ -f "$VCF_FILE" ]; then
       echo "✓ VCF file created: $VCF_FILE"
       echo "  Number of variants: $(zcat $VCF_FILE 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^#' | wc -l || echo 'unable to count')"
   else
       echo "✗ WARNING: VCF file not found!"
       echo "  Contents of population directory:"
       find /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ -type f -name "*.vcf*" 2>/dev/null || echo "  No VCF files found"
   fi

   # Check popmap
   POPMAP_FILE="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv"
   if [ -f "$POPMAP_FILE" ]; then
       echo "✓ Popmap file created: $POPMAP_FILE"
       echo "  Number of individuals: $(wc -l < $POPMAP_FILE)"
       echo "  First 5 lines:"
       head -5 "$POPMAP_FILE"
   else
       echo "✗ WARNING: Popmap file not found!"
   fi
else
   echo "Population simulation failed at $(date)" >&2
   exit 1
fi

echo "=== Population Simulation Complete ==="



Path: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Scripts/radinitio_lib_10_5.sh
This is with the enzyme KpnI
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=rad_lib
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=400G
#SBATCH --time=120:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_lib_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_lib_%j.err

# Create directories
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs

# Thread control
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK

echo "=== Starting Library Simulation at $(date) ==="

# Check if population simulation exists
if [ ! -d "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/msprime_vcfs/" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: msprime_vcfs directory not found!"
   echo "Please run the population simulation first."
   exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ref_loci_vars/ri_master.vcf.gz" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: Population VCF file (ri_master.vcf.gz) not found!"
   echo "Population simulation may not have completed successfully."
   exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: popmap.tsv file not found!"
   echo "The population map file is required for the simulation."
   exit 1
fi

# Create the output directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation

# Run library simulation
radinitio --make-library-seq \
   --genome /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta \
   --out-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/ \
   --make-pop-sim-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ \
   --library-type sdRAD \
   --enz KpnI \
   --coverage 20 \
   --read-length 150 \
   --insert-mean 350 \
   --insert-stdev 37 \
   --lib-bar1-len 6 \
   --read-out-fmt fastq

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   echo "Library simulation completed successfully at $(date)"

   # Find and display barcode file
   BARCODE_FILE=$(find /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/ -name "*barcode*.tsv" -o -name "*barcode*.txt" 2>/dev/null | head -1)

   if [ -n "$BARCODE_FILE" ]; then
       echo ""
       echo "=== BARCODE FILE ==="
       echo "Location: $BARCODE_FILE"
       echo "Total barcodes: $(wc -l < $BARCODE_FILE)"
       echo ""
       echo "First 10 barcodes:"
       head -10 "$BARCODE_FILE"

       # Copy to convenient location
       cp "$BARCODE_FILE" /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/barcodes_for_demultiplexing.tsv
       echo ""
       echo "✓ Barcode file copied to: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_1/barcodes_for_demultiplexing.tsv"
   fi
else
   echo "Library simulation failed at $(date)" >&2
   exit 1
fi

echo "=== Library Simulation Complete ==="

Script: mapping_radinitio_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=map_radinitio_KpnI
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=32gb
#SBATCH --array=1-50
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

# Directories
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/rad_reads"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping"
REF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta"

# Create output directory if needed
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Use the random 100 sample list
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INDIR}/sample_list_10_5_new.txt)

FQ1=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.1.fq.gz
FQ2=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.2.fq.gz
BAM=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

echo "Mapping sample: $SAMPLE"
echo "FQ1: $FQ1"
echo "FQ2: $FQ2"
echo "Output BAM: $BAM"

module purge
module load bio/bwa-mem2/2.2.1-intel-compilers-2023.1.0

bwa-mem2 mem -t 6 $REF $FQ1 $FQ2 > ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam


module purge
module load bio/SAMtools/1.19.2-GCC-13.2.0   # Pick a SAMtools version compatible with GCC

samtools view -bS ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam | samtools sort -o $BAM -
samtools index $BAM

rm ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam



After the mapping, the deduplication was done. Again for every enzyme from 10_5
Script: dedup_radinitio_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=dedup_radinitio
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --array=1-50
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j_%a.err

module purge
module load bio/SAMtools/1.18-GCC-12.3.0

INLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/rad_reads/sample_list_10_5_new.txt"
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup"

mkdir -p $OUTDIR

# Get sample name
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INLIST})

echo "Processing $SAMPLE"

# Input BAM
INBAM=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

# Intermediate + final BAMs
NAME_SORT=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_name_sorted.bam
FIXMATE=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_fixmate.bam
FIXMATE_SORT=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_fixmate_sorted.bam
DEDUP=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_sorted_dedup.bam
STATS=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_duplicates_report.txt

# Step 1: sort by name
samtools sort -n -@ 6 -O bam -o $NAME_SORT $INBAM

# Step 2: fixmate
samtools fixmate -m -O bam $NAME_SORT $FIXMATE
rm -f $NAME_SORT

# Step 3: coordinate sort
samtools sort -@ 6 -O bam -o $FIXMATE_SORT $FIXMATE
rm -f $FIXMATE

# Step 4: mark duplicates (remove them with -r, keep stats with -s)
samtools markdup -r -s -@ 6 $FIXMATE_SORT $DEDUP
rm -f $FIXMATE_SORT

# Step 5: stats summary
samtools flagstat $DEDUP > $STATS

samtools index $DEDUP

echo "Finished $SAMPLE"



This was done for every enzyme from 10_5
Script: multibamqc_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=multibamqc_KpnI
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

BAM_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup"
BAMQC_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/bamqc/"
MULTIBAMQC_DIR="$BAMQC_DIR/multibamqc"

mkdir -p "$BAMQC_DIR" "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR"

# Set Java options for headless mode
export JAVA_OPTS="-Djava.awt.headless=true"

# Run Qualimap BAMQC for each sorted BAM file
for BAM_FILE in "$BAM_DIR"/*_sorted_dedup.bam; do
SAMPLE_NAME=$(basename "$BAM_FILE" _sorted_dedup.bam)
QUALIMAP_OUTPUT_DIR="$BAMQC_DIR/$SAMPLE_NAME"

# Define output and error log files for each sample INSIDE the loop
SBATCH_OUTPUT_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/${SAMPLE_NAME}_bamqc.out"
SBATCH_ERROR_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/${SAMPLE_NAME}_bamqc.err"

echo "Running Qualimap BAMQC for $SAMPLE_NAME"
qualimap bamqc \
-bam "$BAM_FILE" \
-outdir "$QUALIMAP_OUTPUT_DIR" \
-outformat HTML \
-outfile "${SAMPLE_NAME}_report" \
-nw 4 \
> "$SBATCH_OUTPUT_FILE" 2> "$SBATCH_ERROR_FILE"
done

# Prepare input groups file for multi-bamqc
INPUT_GROUPS_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/input_groups.txt"
rm -f "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"

echo "Creating input groups file for Multi-BAMQC"
for BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR in "$BAMQC_DIR"/*; do
if [ -d "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR" ] && [ -f "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR/genome_results.txt" ]; then
SAMPLE_NAME=$(basename "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR")
echo -e "${SAMPLE_NAME}\t${BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR}\t1" >> "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"
fi
done

# Run Qualimap Multi-BAMQC
echo "Running Qualimap Multi-BAMQC"
qualimap multi-bamqc \
-d "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE" \
-outdir "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR" \
-outformat PDF:HTML \
-nw 4

echo "Qualimap analysis complete. Reports saved in $MULTIBAMQC_DIR."



Make the bam_file_paths_sorted.txt from the files in the dedup directory
find "$(pwd)" -maxdepth 1 -name "*_sorted_dedup.bam" | sort > bam_file_paths_sorted.txt
Script: vcf_calling_radinitio_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash

#SBATCH --job-name=vcf_calling_radinitio_KpnI
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

module purge
module load bio/BCFtools/1.19-GCC-13.2.0

# Set paths
# Referencegenome
REF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta"
# List with all the paths to the .sorted.bam files
BAMLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/bam_file_paths_sorted.txt"
# Defines Output where to put the vcf
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"
OUT="${OUTDIR}/KpnI.vcf.gz"

mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Print job info to log file
echo "Starting variant calling with bcftools" > $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Reference Genome: $REF" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "BAM list: $BAMLIST" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Output VCF: $OUT" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log echo "Start time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log

bcftools mpileup \
-f $REF \
-b $BAMLIST \
-q 20 \
-Q 30 \
-a AD,DP,SP \
-Ou \
| bcftools call \
-m -v \
-a GQ,GP \
-Oz \
-o $OUT

echo "End time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log



Script: pre_vis_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=vcftools_stats
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
#SBATCH --mem=8gb
#SBATCH --time=01:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

# Load module
module purge
module load bio/VCFtools/0.1.16-GCC-11.3.0

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/KpnI.vcf.gz"
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcftools"

mkdir -p "$OUT"

echo "Starting VCFtools statistics"
echo "VCF: $SET_VCF"
echo "Output directory: $OUT"

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT



install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"

# Helper to read CSV files

  read.csv(path)
}

var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),

  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_site1_KpnI
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

vcftools    --gzvcf /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/KpnI.vcf.gz \
    --recode-INFO-all \
    --mac 3 \
    --min-alleles 2 \
    --max-alleles 2 \
    --min-meanDP 5 \
    --max-meanDP 40 \
    --minDP 5 \
    --minQ 30 \
    --max-missing 0.8 \
    --out /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80 \
    --recode \
    --remove-indels

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf" 
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80" 

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT

  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID),
  "_msp"
)

colnames(pca) <- c(
  "FID",
  "IID",
  paste0("PC", 1:(ncol(pca)-2))
)

# --- Create clean sample IDs matching popmap.tsv ---

)

)

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=rad_pop   
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=10G
#SBATCH --time=120:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_pop_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_pop_%j.err

# Create directories
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs

# Thread control
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK

echo "=== Starting Population Simulation at $(date) ==="

mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/

# Run population simulation only - FIXED genome path
radinitio --make-population \
   --genome /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta \
   --out-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ \
   --n-pops 10 \
   --n-seq-indv 5

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   echo "Population simulation completed successfully at $(date)"

   # Check outputs
   echo "=== Output files ==="
   ls -lh /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/

   # Check for VCF file
   VCF_FILE="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ref_loci_vars/ri_master.vcf.gz"
   if [ -f "$VCF_FILE" ]; then
       echo "✓ VCF file created: $VCF_FILE"
       echo "  Number of variants: $(zcat $VCF_FILE 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^#' | wc -l || echo 'unable to count')"
   else
       echo "✗ WARNING: VCF file not found!"
       echo "  Contents of population directory:"
       find /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ -type f -name "*.vcf*" 2>/dev/null || echo "  No VCF files found"
   fi

   # Check popmap
   POPMAP_FILE="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv"
   if [ -f "$POPMAP_FILE" ]; then
       echo "✓ Popmap file created: $POPMAP_FILE"
       echo "  Number of individuals: $(wc -l < $POPMAP_FILE)"
       echo "  First 5 lines:"
       head -5 "$POPMAP_FILE"
   else
       echo "✗ WARNING: Popmap file not found!"
   fi
else
   echo "Population simulation failed at $(date)" >&2
   exit 1
fi

echo "=== Population Simulation Complete ==="

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=rad_lib

#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/rad_lib_%j.err

# Create directories
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs

# Thread control
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK

echo "=== Starting Library Simulation at $(date) ==="

# Check if population simulation exists
if [ ! -d "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/msprime_vcfs/" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: msprime_vcfs directory not found!"
   echo "Please run the population simulation first."
   exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ref_loci_vars/ri_master.vcf.gz" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: Population VCF file (ri_master.vcf.gz) not found!"
   echo "Population simulation may not have completed successfully."
   exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv" ]; then
   echo "ERROR: popmap.tsv file not found!"
   echo "The population map file is required for the simulation."
   exit 1
fi

# Create the output directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation

# Run library simulation
radinitio --make-library-seq \
   --genome /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta \
   --out-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/ \
   --make-pop-sim-dir /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/ \
   --library-type sdRAD \
   --enz KpnI \
   --coverage 20 \
   --read-length 150 \
   --insert-mean 350 \
   --insert-stdev 37 \
   --lib-bar1-len 6 \
   --read-out-fmt fastq

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   echo "Library simulation completed successfully at $(date)"

   # Find and display barcode file
   BARCODE_FILE=$(find /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/ -name "*barcode*.tsv" -o -name "*barcode*.txt" 2>/dev/null | head -1)

   if [ -n "$BARCODE_FILE" ]; then
       echo ""
       echo "=== BARCODE FILE ==="
       echo "Location: $BARCODE_FILE"
       echo "Total barcodes: $(wc -l < $BARCODE_FILE)"
       echo ""
       echo "First 10 barcodes:"
       head -10 "$BARCODE_FILE"

       # Copy to convenient location
       cp "$BARCODE_FILE" /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/barcodes_for_demultiplexing.tsv
       echo ""
       echo "✓ Barcode file copied to: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_1/barcodes_for_demultiplexing.tsv"
   fi
else
   echo "Library simulation failed at $(date)" >&2
   exit 1
fi

echo "=== Library Simulation Complete ==="

title: In Silico
text:
The mapping was done for every enzyme for 10_5 with the reference genome of Hypericum pulchrum
Script: mapping_radinitio_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=map_radinitio_KpnI
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=32gb
#SBATCH --array=1-50
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

# Directories
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/rad_reads"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping"
REF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta"

# Create output directory if needed
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Use the random 100 sample list
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INDIR}/sample_list_10_5_new.txt)

FQ1=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.1.fq.gz
FQ2=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.2.fq.gz
BAM=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

echo "Mapping sample: $SAMPLE"
echo "FQ1: $FQ1"
echo "FQ2: $FQ2"
echo "Output BAM: $BAM"

module purge
module load bio/bwa-mem2/2.2.1-intel-compilers-2023.1.0

bwa-mem2 mem -t 6 $REF $FQ1 $FQ2 > ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam


module purge
module load bio/SAMtools/1.19.2-GCC-13.2.0   # Pick a SAMtools version compatible with GCC

samtools view -bS ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam | samtools sort -o $BAM -
samtools index $BAM

rm ${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sam

title: In Silico
text:
After the mapping, the deduplication was done. Again for every enzyme from 10_5
Script: dedup_radinitio_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=dedup_radinitio
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --array=1-50
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j_%a.err

module purge
module load bio/SAMtools/1.18-GCC-12.3.0

INLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/rad_reads/sample_list_10_5_new.txt"
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup"

mkdir -p $OUTDIR

# Get sample name
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INLIST})

echo "Processing $SAMPLE"

# Input BAM
INBAM=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

# Intermediate + final BAMs
NAME_SORT=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_name_sorted.bam
FIXMATE=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_fixmate.bam
FIXMATE_SORT=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_fixmate_sorted.bam
DEDUP=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_sorted_dedup.bam
STATS=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_duplicates_report.txt

# Step 1: sort by name
samtools sort -n -@ 6 -O bam -o $NAME_SORT $INBAM

# Step 2: fixmate
samtools fixmate -m -O bam $NAME_SORT $FIXMATE
rm -f $NAME_SORT

# Step 3: coordinate sort
samtools sort -@ 6 -O bam -o $FIXMATE_SORT $FIXMATE
rm -f $FIXMATE

# Step 4: mark duplicates (remove them with -r, keep stats with -s)
samtools markdup -r -s -@ 6 $FIXMATE_SORT $DEDUP
rm -f $FIXMATE_SORT

# Step 5: stats summary
samtools flagstat $DEDUP > $STATS

samtools index $DEDUP

echo "Finished $SAMPLE"

Script: multibamqc_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=multibamqc_KpnI
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

BAM_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup"
BAMQC_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/bamqc/"
MULTIBAMQC_DIR="$BAMQC_DIR/multibamqc"

mkdir -p "$BAMQC_DIR" "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR"

# Set Java options for headless mode
export JAVA_OPTS="-Djava.awt.headless=true"

# Run Qualimap BAMQC for each sorted BAM file
for BAM_FILE in "$BAM_DIR"/*_sorted_dedup.bam; do
SAMPLE_NAME=$(basename "$BAM_FILE" _sorted_dedup.bam)
QUALIMAP_OUTPUT_DIR="$BAMQC_DIR/$SAMPLE_NAME"

# Define output and error log files for each sample INSIDE the loop
SBATCH_OUTPUT_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/${SAMPLE_NAME}_bamqc.out"
SBATCH_ERROR_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/${SAMPLE_NAME}_bamqc.err"

echo "Running Qualimap BAMQC for $SAMPLE_NAME"
qualimap bamqc \
-bam "$BAM_FILE" \
-outdir "$QUALIMAP_OUTPUT_DIR" \
-outformat HTML \
-outfile "${SAMPLE_NAME}_report" \
-nw 4 \
> "$SBATCH_OUTPUT_FILE" 2> "$SBATCH_ERROR_FILE"
done

# Prepare input groups file for multi-bamqc
INPUT_GROUPS_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/input_groups.txt"
rm -f "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"

echo "Creating input groups file for Multi-BAMQC"
for BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR in "$BAMQC_DIR"/*; do
if [ -d "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR" ] && [ -f "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR/genome_results.txt" ]; then
SAMPLE_NAME=$(basename "$BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR")
echo -e "${SAMPLE_NAME}\t${BAMQC_OUTPUT_DIR}\t1" >> "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"
fi
done

# Run Qualimap Multi-BAMQC
echo "Running Qualimap Multi-BAMQC"
qualimap multi-bamqc \
-d "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE" \
-outdir "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR" \
-outformat PDF:HTML \
-nw 4

echo "Qualimap analysis complete. Reports saved in $MULTIBAMQC_DIR."

Script: vcf_calling_radinitio_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash

#SBATCH --job-name=vcf_calling_radinitio_KpnI
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

module purge
module load bio/BCFtools/1.19-GCC-13.2.0

# Set paths
# Referencegenome
REF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta"
# List with all the paths to the .sorted.bam files
BAMLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/bam_file_paths_sorted.txt"
# Defines Output where to put the vcf
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"
OUT="${OUTDIR}/KpnI.vcf.gz"

mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Print job info to log file
echo "Starting variant calling with bcftools" > $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Reference Genome: $REF" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "BAM list: $BAMLIST" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Output VCF: $OUT" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log echo "Start time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log

bcftools mpileup \
-f $REF \
-b $BAMLIST \
-q 20 \
-Q 30 \
-a AD,DP,SP \
-Ou \
| bcftools call \
-m -v \
-a GQ,GP \
-Oz \
-o $OUT

echo "End time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log

title: In Silico
text:
Script: pre_vis_KpnI.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=vcftools_stats
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
#SBATCH --mem=8gb
#SBATCH --time=01:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

# Load module
module purge
module load bio/VCFtools/0.1.16-GCC-11.3.0

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/KpnI.vcf.gz"
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcftools"

mkdir -p "$OUT"

echo "Starting VCFtools statistics"
echo "VCF: $SET_VCF"
echo "Output directory: $OUT"

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT

install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.lqual"),
c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.ldepth.mean"),
c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.lmiss"),
c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_raw <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.frq"),
c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))
parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))
var_freq <- var_freq_raw %>%
mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
maf = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.idepth"),
c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.imiss"),
c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.het"),
c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F")) # F ~ inbreeding coefficient

write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Patchwork Plot

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))

ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {

if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +

geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly (avoids relying on | and / operators)
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_raw_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_raw_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9

# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for raw VCF")

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_site1_KpnI
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

vcftools    --gzvcf /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/KpnI.vcf.gz \
    --recode-INFO-all \
    --mac 3 \
    --min-alleles 2 \
    --max-alleles 2 \
    --min-meanDP 5 \
    --max-meanDP 40 \
    --minDP 5 \
    --minQ 30 \
    --max-missing 0.8 \
    --out /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80 \
    --recode \
    --remove-indels

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf" 
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80" 

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT

title: In Silico
text:
bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf | awk -F'/' '{print $NF}' | awk -F'.sorted.bam' '{print $1}' | awk -F'_' '{print $2 "_" $1 "_" $3}' > new_sample_names.txt

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf | awk -F'/' '{print $NF}' | awk -F'.sorted.bam' '{print $1}' | awk -F'_' '{print $2 "_" $1 "_" $4}' > new_sample_names.txt

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf > old_sample_names.txt

paste old_sample_names.txt new_sample_names.txt > rename_samples.txt

bcftools reheader -s rename_samples.txt minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80.recode.vcf -o minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_shortnames.vcf

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_shortnames.vcf

plink --vcf minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_shortnames.vcf --double-id --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80

plink --bfile minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80 --pca --allow-extra-chr --out minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_pca
install.packages("readr")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("ggrepel")
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(ggrepel)

# --- Read eigenvectors ---
pca <- read_table(
"/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/simulation/mapping/dedup/vcf/vcf_filtered/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss80_pca.eigenvec",
col_names = FALSE
)

colnames(pca) <- c(
  "FID",
  "IID",
  paste0("PC", 1:(ncol(pca)-2))
)

# --- Create clean sample IDs matching popmap.tsv ---
pca$sample <- paste0(
  "msp_",
  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID)
)

# --- Create short labels like 00_msp ---
pca$label <- paste0(
  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID),
  "_msp"
)

# --- Read population assignments ---
colnames(pca) <- c(
  "FID",
  "IID",
  paste0("PC", 1:(ncol(pca)-2))
)

# --- Create clean sample IDs matching popmap.tsv ---
pca$sample <- paste0(
  "msp_",
  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID)
)

# --- Create short labels like 00_msp ---
pca$label <- paste0(
  sub("_.*", "", pca$FID),
  "_msp"
)

popmap <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/radinitio/hypericumpulchrum/population/10_5/popmap.tsv",
  col_names = c("sample", "group")
)

# --- Merge into PCA table ---
pca <- left_join(pca, popmap, by = "sample")

# --- Colors for groups ---
library(scales)

group_levels <- unique(pca$group)

group_colors <- setNames(
  hue_pal()(length(group_levels)),
  group_levels
)

pca_plot <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC2, color = group)) +
geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
theme_bw() +
labs(title = "PCA PC1-PC2")

# --- Save to file directly ---
ggsave(

Script: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Scripts/demultiplexing_old_hpu.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash

#SBATCH --job-name=demux_HM
#SBATCH --time=30:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=400G
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/logs/%x_%j.err

# ============================================================
# PURPOSE
# ============================================================
# This pipeline:
#
# 1. Trims the 5 bp spacer from R1 only
# 2. Demultiplexes reads using process_radtags
# 3. Optionally removes PCR duplicates AFTER demultiplexing
#
# Library structure identified:
#
#   NNNNN + BARCODE + GGTACC + genomic DNA
#
# where:
#   NNNNN = diversity spacer (5 bp)
#   BARCODE = inline barcode (6 bp)
#   GGTACC = KpnI restriction site
#
# ============================================================

set -euo pipefail
shopt -s nullglob

# ============================================================
# LOAD MODULES
# ============================================================

module purge

module load bio/Stacks/2.62-foss-2022a
module load bio/cutadapt/4.2-GCCcore-11.3.0

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================

THREADS=8

FASTQ_DIR="/scratch/RADseq_ag-deamux/NGS_24D324"
OUTROOT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old"
mkdir -p "$OUTROOT"

BARCODE_FILE="$FASTQ_DIR/barcodes_pool4.txt"

R1="$FASTQ_DIR/DX166_303714_24D324-4_S56_L000_R1_001.fastq.gz"
R2="$FASTQ_DIR/DX166_303714_24D324-4_S56_L000_R2_001.fastq.gz"

ENZYME="kpnI"

# process_radtags options
INLINE_FLAG="--inline_null"
BARCODE_MISMATCH="--barcode-dist-1 1"

# ============================================================
# OUTPUT DIRECTORIES
# ============================================================

LOG_DIR="$OUTROOT/logs"
TRIM_DIR="$OUTROOT/trimmed"
RAD_DIR="$OUTROOT/radtags"
DUP_DIR="$OUTROOT/clone_filtered"

mkdir -p \
    "$LOG_DIR" \
    "$TRIM_DIR" \
    "$RAD_DIR" \
    "$DUP_DIR"

# ============================================================
# CHECK INPUT FILES
# ============================================================

if [[ ! -f "$R1" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: R1 file not found"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$R2" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: R2 file not found"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$BARCODE_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: Barcode file not found"
    exit 1
fi

echo "=================================================="
echo "JOB STARTED: $(date)"
echo "HOSTNAME: $(hostname)"
echo "=================================================="

# ============================================================
# STEP 1 — TRIM 5 bp SPACER FROM R1 ONLY
# ============================================================
#
# Your data show:
#
#   position 6 = barcode
#   position 12 = GGTACC
#
# meaning:
#
#   5 bp spacer + 6 bp barcode + restriction site
#
# We therefore remove first 5 bp from R1 only.
#
# R2 remains untouched.
#
# ============================================================

echo "=================================================="
echo "STEP 1: TRIMMING R1 SPACER"
echo "=================================================="

TRIM_R1="$TRIM_DIR/trimmed_R1.fastq.gz"
TRIM_R2="$TRIM_DIR/trimmed_R2.fastq.gz"

cutadapt \
    -u 5 \
    -o "$TRIM_R1" \
    -p "$TRIM_R2" \
    "$R1" \
    "$R2" \
    > "$LOG_DIR/cutadapt.log" 2>&1

echo "Spacer trimming completed"

# ============================================================
# STEP 2 — DEMULTIPLEX WITH process_radtags
# ============================================================

echo "=================================================="
echo "STEP 2: process_radtags"
echo "=================================================="

process_radtags \
    -1 "$TRIM_R1" \
    -2 "$TRIM_R2" \
    -o "$RAD_DIR" \
    -b "$BARCODE_FILE" \
    -e "$ENZYME" \
    $INLINE_FLAG \
    $BARCODE_MISMATCH \
    -r -c -q \
    -i gzfastq \
    -T "$THREADS" \
    -D \
    > "$LOG_DIR/process_radtags.log" 2>&1

echo "process_radtags completed"

# ============================================================
# STEP 3 — OPTIONAL PCR DUPLICATE REMOVAL
# ============================================================
#
# This is performed AFTER demultiplexing,
# which is the biologically cleaner workflow.
#
# ============================================================

echo "=================================================="
echo "STEP 3: clone_filter"
echo "=================================================="

DEMUX_R1=("$RAD_DIR"/*.1.fq.gz)

if [[ ${#DEMUX_R1[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: No demultiplexed files found"
    exit 1
fi

for r1_file in "${DEMUX_R1[@]}"; do

    sample=$(basename "$r1_file" .1.fq.gz)

    r2_file="$RAD_DIR/${sample}.2.fq.gz"

    if [[ ! -f "$r2_file" ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Missing R2 for sample $sample"
        continue
    fi

    sample_out="$DUP_DIR/$sample"

    mkdir -p "$sample_out"

    echo "Processing clone_filter for sample: $sample"

    clone_filter \
        -1 "$r1_file" \
        -2 "$r2_file" \
        -o "$sample_out" \
        -i gzfastq \
        --oligo-len-1 6 \
        -y gzfastq \
        > "$sample_out/clone_filter.log" 2>&1
done

echo "=================================================="
echo "PIPELINE FINISHED SUCCESSFULLY"
echo "DATE: $(date)"
echo "=================================================="





{bash}
# Extract the per_barcode_raw_read_counts section and create a TSV
sed -n '/BEGIN per_barcode_raw_read_counts/,/END per_barcode_raw_read_counts/p' /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/radtags/process_radtags.trimmed.log | \
grep -v "BEGIN\|END" > /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/radtags/per_sample_counts.tsv
After that, make the figures for the Proportion of Library, Retained Reads (M) and Proportion of Retained Reads



{r}

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Plot Stacks-Process Radtags per-sample statistics

## Input data

### Path to output PDFs
pdfPath = '/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/pdf'

### Path to `per_sample_counts.tsv` file
procRadtagsLogPath = '/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/radtags/per_sample_counts.tsv'

### Open the file as a dataframe
processRadtags = read.delim(procRadtagsLogPath)

### Check column names
print(colnames(processRadtags))  # Optional debug

### Add the population identifier (extract it from the sample names)
processRadtags$PopID <- sapply(strsplit(processRadtags$Filename, '_'),
                               function(x){x[2]})

## Calculate overall statistics
totalReads <- sum(processRadtags$Total)
totalRetained <- sum(processRadtags$Retained.Reads)
percRetained <- totalRetained / totalReads

## Plots

### Initialize PDF
pdf(paste0(pdfPath, '/process_radtags_stats.pdf'), width=8, height=8)

### A. Proportion of Library

#### Calculate percentage of total reads
processRadtags$PercTotal <- processRadtags$Total / totalReads
summary(processRadtags$PercTotal)
sd(processRadtags$PercTotal)

#### Sort the dataframe by the percent total
processRadtags <- processRadtags[order(processRadtags$PercTotal),]

#### Plot dotchart
dotchart(processRadtags$PercTotal,
         labels = processRadtags$Filename,
         xlab='',
         xlim=c(0,0.08))
title(xlab='Proportion of Library', line=2)
abline(v=mean(processRadtags$PercTotal), lty=2, col='#b22222')

#### Per-population Boxplot
boxplot(PercTotal~PopID, data=processRadtags,
        las=1,
        xlab='',
        ylab='',
        col=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9"),
        horizontal = TRUE,
        ylim=c(0,0.08))
title(xlab='Proportion of Library', line=2)
title(ylab='Population', line=2)

### B. Reads per-sample

#### Stats
summary(processRadtags$Retained.Reads)
sd(processRadtags$Retained.Reads)

#### Sort
processRadtags <- processRadtags[order(processRadtags$Retained.Reads),]

#### Dotchart
dotchart(processRadtags$Retained.Reads / 1e6,
         labels = processRadtags$Filename,
         xlab='',
         xlim=c(0, max(processRadtags$Retained.Reads) / 1e6))
title(xlab='Retained Reads (M)', line=2)
abline(v=mean(processRadtags$Retained.Reads / 1e6), lty=2, col='#b22222')

#### Per-population boxplot
par(mar=c(3,3,0.5,0.5) + 0.1)
boxplot(Retained.Reads / 1e6 ~ PopID, data=processRadtags,
        las=1,
        xlab='',
        ylab='',
        col=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9"),
        horizontal = TRUE,
        ylim=c(0, max(processRadtags$Retained.Reads / 1e6)))
title(xlab='Retained Reads (M)', line=2)
title(ylab='Population', line=2)

### C. Percent retained

#### Calculate percent retained
processRadtags$PercRetained <- processRadtags$Retained.Reads / processRadtags$Total
summary(processRadtags$PercRetained)
sd(processRadtags$PercRetained)

#### Sort
processRadtags <- processRadtags[order(processRadtags$PercRetained),]

#### Dotchart
dotchart(processRadtags$PercRetained,
         labels = processRadtags$Filename,
         xlab='',
         xlim=c(0.4,1))
title(xlab='Proportion of Retained Reads', line=2)
abline(v=mean(processRadtags$PercRetained), lty=2, col='#b22222')

#### Per-population Boxplot
boxplot(PercRetained~PopID, data=processRadtags,
        las=1,
        xlab='',
        ylab='',
        col=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9"),
        horizontal = TRUE,
        ylim=c(0.4,1))
title(xlab='Proportion of Retained Reads', line=2)
title(ylab='Population', line=2)

### Close PDF
dev.off()

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=fastp_HM
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16G
#SBATCH --time=02:00:00
#SBATCH --array=1-20
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/logs/%x_%A_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/logs/%x_%A_%a.err

# --------------------
# Directories
# --------------------
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/radtags"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/fastp"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# --------------------
# Build sample list from forward reads
# --------------------
SAMPLE_LIST=($(ls ${INDIR}/*.1.fq.gz | grep -v '\.rem' | xargs -n1 basename | sed 's/\.1\.fq\.gz//'))
SAMPLE=${SAMPLE_LIST[$SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID-1]}

# --------------------
# Input files
# --------------------
FQ1=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.1.fq.gz
FQ2=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.2.fq.gz

# --------------------
# Output files
# --------------------
OUT1=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_R1.fastp.fq.gz
OUT2=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_R2.fastp.fq.gz
HTML=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.fastp.html
JSON=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.fastp.json

# --------------------
# Run FastP
# --------------------
fastp \
  -i "$FQ1" \
  -I "$FQ2" \
  -o "$OUT1" \
  -O "$OUT2" \
  --detect_adapter_for_pe \
  --thread $SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK \
  --qualified_quality_phred 20 \
  --length_required 50 \
  --dedup \
  --html "$HTML" \
  --json "$JSON"
module load bio/MultiQC
multiqc . -o fastp_summary

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=fastqc_clone_old
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/logs/%x_%j.err

module purge
module load bio/FastQC

BASE_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/clone_filtered"
OUT_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/clone_filtered/fastqc"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

for SAMPLE_DIR in "$BASE_DIR"/*; do
    # Only directories, skip *.rem
    if [[ -d "$SAMPLE_DIR" ]] && [[ "$SAMPLE_DIR" != *.rem ]]; then
        echo "Processing directory: $SAMPLE_DIR"
        
        # Run FastQC on all .fq.gz files inside
        for FQ in "$SAMPLE_DIR"/*.fq.gz; do
            # Check if files exist
            if [[ -f "$FQ" ]]; then
                echo "  Running FastQC on $FQ"
                fastqc -t 4 -o "$OUT_DIR" "$FQ"
            fi
        done
    fi
done

dedup_HM.sh
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=dedup_hm_old
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%j_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%j_%a.err
#SBATCH --array=1-51

module purge
module load bio/SAMtools/1.18-GCC-12.3.0

INLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/fastp/sample_list_HM.txt"
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/mapping"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/mapping/dedup"

mkdir -p $OUTDIR

# Get sample name
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INLIST})

echo "Processing $SAMPLE"

# Input BAM
INBAM=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

# Intermediate + final BAMs
NAME_SORT=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_name_sorted.bam
FIXMATE=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_fixmate.bam
FIXMATE_SORT=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_fixmate_sorted.bam
DEDUP=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_sorted_dedup.bam
STATS=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_duplicates_report.txt

# Step 1: sort by name
samtools sort -n -@ 6 -O bam -o $NAME_SORT $INBAM

# Step 2: fixmate
samtools fixmate -m -O bam $NAME_SORT $FIXMATE
rm -f $NAME_SORT

# Step 3: coordinate sort
samtools sort -@ 6 -O bam -o $FIXMATE_SORT $FIXMATE
rm -f $FIXMATE

# Step 4: mark duplicates (remove them with -r, keep stats with -s)
samtools markdup -r -s -@ 6 $FIXMATE_SORT $DEDUP
rm -f $FIXMATE_SORT

# Step 5: stats summary
samtools flagstat $DEDUP > $STATS

samtools index $DEDUP

echo "Finished $SAMPLE"

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=multibamqc_hm_old
#SBATCH --time=0:15:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
#SBATCH --mem=8gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%A.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%A.err

set -euo pipefail

# CORRECTED PATH: Points exactly to where you ran 'ls'
BAMQC_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/mapping/dedup/bamqc"
MULTIBAMQC_DIR="$BAMQC_DIR/multibamqc"
INPUT_GROUPS_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/input_groups.txt"

# Create the final summary directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR"
rm -f "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"

echo "Locating existing BAMQC reports and creating input groups file..."

# Loop through your existing folders (like 216_HM_10, 271_HM_3, etc.)
for PATH_DIR in "$BAMQC_DIR"/*; do
    # Verify it's a directory, ignore the target output folder, and confirm genome_results.txt exists
    if [ -d "$PATH_DIR" ] && [ "$(basename "$PATH_DIR")" != "multibamqc" ] && [ -f "$PATH_DIR/genome_results.txt" ]; then
        NAME=$(basename "$PATH_DIR")
        echo -e "${NAME}\t${PATH_DIR}\t1" >> "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"
    fi
done

echo "Running Qualimap Multi-BAMQC on existing data..."
export JAVA_OPTS="-Djava.awt.headless=true"

qualimap multi-bamqc \
    -d "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE" \
    -outdir "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR" \
    -outformat PDF:HTML \
    -nw 400

echo "Qualimap Multi-BAMQC summary report generation complete!"

BAMLIST_OLD="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/mapping/dedup/bam_file_paths_sorted_old.txt"
BAMLIST_NEW="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/mapping/dedup/bam_file_paths_sorted_new.txt"
BAMLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/bamlist.txt"

cat "$BAMLIST_OLD" "$BAMLIST_NEW" > "$BAMLIST"

echo "Combined BAM count: $(wc -l < "$BAMLIST")" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log

OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM"

OUT="${OUTDIR}/AlandIslandsHM.vcf.gz"

mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Print job info to log file
echo "Starting variant calling with bcftools" > $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Reference Genome: $REF" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "BAM list: $BAMLIST" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Output VCF: $OUT" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Start time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log

bcftools mpileup \
-f "$REF" \
-b "$BAMLIST" \
-q 20 \
-Q 30 \
-a AD,DP,SP \
-Ou \
| bcftools call \
-m -v \
-a GQ,GP \
-Oz \
-o "$OUT"

bcftools index "$OUT"

echo "End time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log



After this, I did the pre-visualisation for the data to be able to make the patchwork plots for the VCF file.
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=vcftools_stats
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
#SBATCH --mem=8gb
#SBATCH --time=01:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

# Load module
module purge
module load bio/VCFtools/0.1.16-GCC-11.3.0

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/AlandIslandsHM.vcf.gz"
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

mkdir -p "$OUT"

echo "Starting VCFtools statistics"
echo "VCF: $SET_VCF"
echo "Output directory: $OUT"

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT



install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.lqual"),
c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.ldepth.mean"),
c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.lmiss"),
c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_raw <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.frq"),
c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))
parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))
var_freq <- var_freq_raw %>%
mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
maf = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.idepth"),
c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.imiss"),
c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.het"),
c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F")) # F ~ inbreeding coefficient

write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Patchwork Plot

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <-  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---

var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))

ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {

}

# Create plots for each dataset

geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly (avoids relying on | and / operators)
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


#save panel as pdf file
ggsave( "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_raw_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)



library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_raw_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_raw_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv2.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv2.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv2.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv2.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv2_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv2_stats_summary.tsv"
)

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_site2
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

vcftools    --vcf /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/INDMISS20.recode.vcf \
    --recode-INFO-all \
    --mac 3 \
    --min-alleles 2 \
    --max-alleles 2 \
    --min-meanDP 5 \
    --max-meanDP 40 \
    --minDP 5 \
    --minQ 30 \
    --max-missing 0.9 \
    --out /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20 \
    --recode \
    --remove-indels

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.recode.vcf" 
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20" 

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT 



# VISUALISATION OF THE FILTERED VCF STATS

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site2.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site2.csv"))

  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),

  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),

) |>

#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_indv3
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/PCA_ready_indfiltered.recode.vcf" 
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/PCA_ready_indfiltered" 

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT

# Define file paths

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
  vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.lqual"),
                     c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.ldepth.mean"),
                      c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.lmiss"),
                     c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_filtered <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.frq"),
                         c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))

parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))

var_freq <- var_freq_filtered %>%
  mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
         freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
         maf   = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.idepth"),
                      c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss  <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.imiss"),
                      c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het   <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.het"),
                      c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F"))  # F ~ inbreeding coefficient
                      
write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv3.csv"))

install.packages("readr")

# --- Read eigenvectors ---

    "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20_pca.eigenvec", 
    col_names = FALSE
    )

# Check the new column
pca[, c("FID", "group")]


library(RColorBrewer)

)
